content:
peter197 said
I keep entering my details but it does not work, I have sent the tested the settings and they are OK nbut the system keeps asking me to re enter password .I have not changed any passwords and only want a pop3 account to send and receive <peter@hainsworthsboatyard.co.uk> I have my own server and domain name through Maxer via C panel .I use microsoft outlook OK but cannot alter my e mail box size

I have to ask. Is your account in outlook configured as IMAP or POP? I think we need to look at the basic original issue before moving on to Thunderbird and it's issues. I think they may be related, but it is only a gut feeling.

So check the account in cpanel. How much quota is available there? See https://my.maxer.com/knowledgebase/60/How-to-set-the-storage-space-limit-of-an-email-account-or-mailbox.html

It does sounds to me like the cpanel quota is exhausted and your outlook is bucking about it and Thunderbird can not create an account for you because the server is refusing to play along because the account has a full quota situation. It would certainly explain the issues in Outlook. It is a stretch for Thunderbird. Quota is a server side setting so neither outlook or Thunderbird would be able to modify it.

.
creator: MattAuSupport

content:
Windows OS has a long running issue when it comes to using 'Send to' > 'Mail Recipient' or 'Send to' > 'Thunderbird'

It's not worked for me for ages. Microsoft help forum sites have tons of reports on this going back years. If I have an image file and use : 'Send to' > 'Mail Recipient' - nothing happens 'Send to' > 'Thunderbird' - Windows OS chooses to open it in Photos and nothing is added to email.

Then Windows starts running hard because the fixmapi.exe file repeatedly is invoked. I can see ti in the Task Manager. I have to reboot the computer to disable it.

I just create email and add attachment because I know it works every time. Less frustrating,

But you could try this which did work for one person: Set winword to run as administrator then reset winword so as NOT to run as administrator. And after I did that, I no longer got the MAPI failure.

But if using a 'share' option - it's been reported that 'word' only shares to 'outlook', MS has control over this.

creator: ervandpeg

content:
Ok, I do not know MAC, but this always works:

  1. click help>troubleshootinginformation
  2. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder;
  3. exit thunderbird
  4. you should now be in the MAC file manager
  5. back up one folder
  6. now, copy that folder elsewhere, preferably to external media, but anywhere other than where it is.

Should you need to restore:

  1. click help>troubeshootinginformation
  2. scroll to 'profile folder' and click 'open folder'
  3. exit thunderbird
  4. delete everything in the folder
  5. now, open your backup folder and paste everything in that folder here
  6. restart thunderbird

Note that some of the steps are the same but not all. :)
